text_original: Motse said : WitE the passing of the sage-kings of the Three Djhasties, the world lost its righteousness and the feudal lords took might as right.
text_normalized: Motse said : WitE the passing of the sage-kings of the Three Djhasties, the world lost its righteousness and the feudal lords took might as right.

text_original: The superior and the subordinates' are no longer gracious and loyal ; father and son, elder and younger brother are no longer affectionate and filial, brotherly and respectful, virtuous and kind.
text_normalized: The superior and the subordinates' are no longer gracious and loyal ; father and son, elder and younger brother are no longer affectionate and filial, brotherly and respectful, virtuous and kind.

text_original: The rulers do not attend diligently to government and the artisans do not attend earnestly to their work.
text_normalized: The rulers do not attend diligently to government and the artisans do not attend earnestly to their work.

text_original: The people practise immorality and wickedness and become rebellious.
text_normalized: The people practise immorality and wickedness and become rebellious.

text_original: Thieves and bandits with weapons, poison, water, and fire hold up innocent travellers on the highways and the bypaths, robbing them of their carts and horses, coats and fur coats, to enrich themselves.
text_normalized: Thieves and bandits with weapons, poison, water, and fire hold up innocent travellers on the highways and the bypaths, robbing them of their carts and horses, coats and fur coats, to enrich themselves.

text_original: All these start therewith (with the passing of the sage-kings).
text_normalized: All these start therewith (with the passing of the sage-kings).

text_original: And so the world falls into chaos.
text_normalized: And so the world falls into chaos.

text_original: How what is the reason for this confusion ?
text_normalized: How what is the reason for this confusion ?

text_original: It is all because of the doubt of the existence of the ghosts and spirits, and the ignorance of their being able to reward virtue and punish vice.
text_normalized: It is all because of the doubt of the existence of the ghosts and spirits, and the ignorance of their being able to reward virtue and punish vice.

text_original: If all the people in the world believed that the spirits are able to reward virtue and punish vice, how could the world be in chaos ?
text_normalized: If all the people in the world believed that the spirits are able to reward virtue and punish vice, how could the world be in chaos ?

text_original: Those who deny the existence of spirits proclaim : Of course there are no spirits.'' And from morning till evening they teach this doctrine to the people of the empire.
text_normalized: Those who deny the existence of spirits proclaim : Of course there are no spirits.'' And from morning till evening they teach this doctrine to the people of the empire.

text_original: They bewilder the people, causing them all to doubt the existence of ghosts and spirits.
text_normalized: They bewilder the people, causing them all to doubt the existence of ghosts and spirits.

text_original: In ON GHOSTS (HI) tliis way tke empire becomes disorderly.
text_normalized: In ON GHOSTS (HI) tliis way tke empire becomes disorderly.

text_original: Tlierefore Motse said : If the rnlers and the gentlemen of the world really desire to procure benefits for the empire and remove its calamities they must understand whether ghosts -and spirits exist.
text_normalized: Tlierefore Motse said : If the rnlers and the gentlemen of the world really desire to procure benefits for the empire and remove its calamities they must understand whether ghosts -and spirits exist.

text_original: or not, ' Since we must understand whether ghosts and spirits exist or notj how can we find out ?
text_normalized: or not, ' Since we must understand whether ghosts and spirits exist or notj how can we find out ?

text_original: Motse said : The yay to find out whether anything exists or not is to depend on the testimony of the ears and eyes of the multitude.
text_normalized: Motse said : The yay to find out whether anything exists or not is to depend on the testimony of the ears and eyes of the multitude.

text_original: If some have heard it or some have seen it then we have to say it exists.
text_normalized: If some have heard it or some have seen it then we have to say it exists.

text_original: If no one has heard it and no one has seen it then we have to say it does not exist.
text_normalized: If no one has heard it and no one has seen it then we have to say it does not exist.

text_original: So, then, why not go to some village or some district and inquire ?
text_normalized: So, then, why not go to some village or some district and inquire ?

text_original: If from antiquity to the present, and since the beginning of man, there are men •vfho have seen the bodies of ghosts and spirits and heard their voice, how can we say that they do not exist ?
text_normalized: If from antiquity to the present, and since the beginning of man, there are men •vfho have seen the bodies of ghosts and spirits and heard their voice, how can we say that they do not exist ?

text_original: If none have heard them and none have seen them, then how can we say they do ?
text_normalized: If none have heard them and none have seen them, then how can we say they do ?

text_original: But those who deny the existence of the spirits say : Many in the world have heard and seen something of ghosts and spirits.
text_normalized: But those who deny the existence of the spirits say : Many in the world have heard and seen something of ghosts and spirits.

text_original: (Since they vary in their testimony,) who are to be accepted as really having heard and seen them ?
text_normalized: (Since they vary in their testimony,) who are to be accepted as really having heard and seen them ?

text_original: Motse said : As we are to rely on what many have jointly seen and what many have jointly heard, the case of Tu Po is (to be accepted).
text_normalized: Motse said : As we are to rely on what many have jointly seen and what many have jointly heard, the case of Tu Po is (to be accepted).

text_original: ’’ Motse said : If the senses of hearing and sight of the multitude are thought to be not trustworthy, we may ask if such men like the sage-kings of the Three Dynasties, Yao, Shim, Yii, T'ang, Wen, and Wu, are trustworthy ?

text_original: Of course, about this all people above the mediocre will say such men like the ancient sage-kings of the Three Dynasties, Yao, Shim, Yii, T’ang, Wen, and Wu, are trustworthy.

text_original: Yet they were afraid their descendants might not understand it.

text_original: He said : , ' The Prince of' Pin violated the five elements and, disused the three calendars.
text_normalized: He said : , ' The Prince of' Pin violated the five elements and, disused the three calendars.

text_original: Heaven decreed to exterminate his life !
text_normalized: Heaven decreed to exterminate his life !

text_original: ' And he continued : " At noon I shall grapple with the Prince of Hu for the fate of the day.
text_normalized: ' And he continued : " At noon I shall grapple with the Prince of Hu for the fate of the day.

text_original: But (mind yoii)j you ministers and people, it is not because I covet their land and treasures.
text_normalized: But (mind yoii)j you ministers and people, it is not because I covet their land and treasures.

text_original: I am only carrying out the punish-ment in obedience to Heaven.
text_normalized: I am only carrying out the punish-ment in obedience to Heaven.

text_original: If you on the left do not do your part on the left you will be disobeying my orders ; if you on the right do not do your part on the right you will be disobeying my orders ; if you charioteers do not manage your horses according to orders you will be disobeying my orders.
text_normalized: If you on the left do not do your part on the left you will be disobeying my orders ; if you on the right do not do your part on the right you will be disobeying my orders ; if you charioteers do not manage your horses according to orders you will be disobeying my orders.

text_original: And rewards wdll be distributed before my ancestors and punishments will be meted out before the altar/  Why were rewards distributed before the ancestors ?
text_normalized: And rewards wdll be distributed before my ancestors and punishments will be meted out before the altar/  Why were rewards distributed before the ancestors ?

text_original: To submit their fairness.
text_normalized: To submit their fairness.

text_original: Why were punish-ments meted out before the altar 1 To submit their justice.
text_normalized: Why were punish-ments meted out before the altar 1 To submit their justice.

text_original: Because the ancient sage-kings must reward virtue and punish vice with ghosts and spirits, they distributed rewards before the ancestors and meted out punishments before the altar.
text_normalized: Because the ancient sage-kings must reward virtue and punish vice with ghosts and spirits, they distributed rewards before the ancestors and meted out punishments before the altar.

text_original: And here we have a testimony of ghosts in the books of Hsia.
text_normalized: And here we have a testimony of ghosts in the books of Hsia.

text_original: Now, first in the books of Hsia and next in the books of Shang and Chou, testimonies to the existence of ghosts and spirits occur again and again.
text_normalized: Now, first in the books of Hsia and next in the books of Shang and Chou, testimonies to the existence of ghosts and spirits occur again and again.

text_original: What is the reason for this ?
text_normalized: What is the reason for this ?

text_original: Because the sage-kings were interested in it.
text_normalized: Because the sage-kings were interested in it.

text_original: Judging from the records of these books, how can we doubt that there are ghosts and spirits ? pp.
text_normalized: Judging from the records of these books, how can we doubt that there are ghosts and spirits ? pp.

text_original: Anciently, on the propitions day of ting mao, Chon offered thanlrsgiving to Earth and the Four Quarters, and their ancestors.
text_normalized: Anciently, on the propitions day of ting mao, Chon offered thanlrsgiving to Earth and the Four Quarters, and their ancestors.

text_original: They did this to prolong their age.
text_normalized: They did this to prolong their age.

text_original: If there were no ghosts and spirits, how could their age be prolonged ?
text_normalized: If there were no ghosts and spirits, how could their age be prolonged ?

text_original: Motse said: As to the fact that ghosts and spirits can reward virtue as well as punish vice, if it could be pro-claimed to the whole country and to all the people it would really he a source of orderliness in the country and blessing to the people.
text_normalized: Motse said: As to the fact that ghosts and spirits can reward virtue as well as punish vice, if it could be pro-claimed to the whole country and to all the people it would really he a source of orderliness in the country and blessing to the people.

text_original: The corruption of the officials in their public charges and the immorality among men and women will all be seen by ghosts and spirits.
text_normalized: The corruption of the officials in their public charges and the immorality among men and women will all be seen by ghosts and spirits.

text_original: The vice of those who, with weapons, poisons, and water and jSire, waylay innocent travellers and rob them of their carts and horses, coats and fur coats to enrich themselves will be seen by ghosts and spirits.
text_normalized: The vice of those who, with weapons, poisons, and water and jSire, waylay innocent travellers and rob them of their carts and horses, coats and fur coats to enrich themselves will be seen by ghosts and spirits.

text_original: Thereupon the officials will not dare be corrupt in office, withholding reward when they find the virtuous or withholding punishment when they find the wiched.
text_normalized: Thereupon the officials will not dare be corrupt in office, withholding reward when they find the virtuous or withholding punishment when they find the wiched.

text_original: And those among the people who commit vice and cruelties and with weapons, poisons, and water and fire waylay the innocent travellers, robbing them of their carts and horses, coats and fur coats to enrich themselves — all these will be no more.
text_normalized: And those among the people who commit vice and cruelties and with weapons, poisons, and water and fire waylay the innocent travellers, robbing them of their carts and horses, coats and fur coats to enrich themselves — all these will be no more.

text_original: And the world will have order.
text_normalized: And the world will have order.

text_original: Really the intelligence of the ghosts and spirits cannot be combated.
text_normalized: Really the intelligence of the ghosts and spirits cannot be combated.

text_original: Even in solitary caves, big ponds, woods and valleys, the ghosts and spirits are watching.
text_normalized: Even in solitary caves, big ponds, woods and valleys, the ghosts and spirits are watching.

text_original: And the punishments from ghosts and spirits cannot be evaded.
text_normalized: And the punishments from ghosts and spirits cannot be evaded.

text_original: Even wealth and great numbers, daring and strength, strong armour and sharp weapons, the punishment of ghosts and spirits will frustrate.
text_normalized: Even wealth and great numbers, daring and strength, strong armour and sharp weapons, the punishment of ghosts and spirits will frustrate.

text_original: If this is doubted, look at the story of the ancient King Chieh of Hsia.
text_normalized: If this is doubted, look at the story of the ancient King Chieh of Hsia.

text_original: He was an emperor in honour and possessed the whole empire in wealth.
text_normalized: He was an emperor in honour and possessed the whole empire in wealth.

text_original: He cursed Heaven and blasphemed against the spirits above and destroyed the multitudes below.
text_normalized: He cursed Heaven and blasphemed against the spirits above and destroyed the multitudes below.

text_original: Thereupon Heaven commissioned T’ang to carry out the judicious punishment.
text_normalized: Thereupon Heaven commissioned T’ang to carry out the judicious punishment.

text_original: With nine chariots  T’ang arranged the Bird Formation and the Wild Goose March.
text_normalized: With nine chariots  T’ang arranged the Bird Formation and the Wild Goose March.

text_original: He climbed the Ta Tsan  and scattered the forces of Hsia and entered its land.
text_normalized: He climbed the Ta Tsan  and scattered the forces of Hsia and entered its land.

text_original: And he captured T’uei Yi Ta Hsi.
text_normalized: And he captured T’uei Yi Ta Hsi.

text_original: Now, King Chieh of Hsia was an emperor in honour and possessed the whole empire in wealth.
text_normalized: Now, King Chieh of Hsia was an emperor in honour and possessed the whole empire in wealth.

text_original: In his service was the man of great daring and strength, T'uei Yi Ta Hsi, who had torn apart a buffalo alive.
text_normalized: In his service was the man of great daring and strength, T'uei Yi Ta Hsi, who had torn apart a buffalo alive.

text_original: He could kill a man at the move of a finger, and the number of those killed amounted to a million, and they were thrown into lakes and mountains.
text_normalized: He could kill a man at the move of a finger, and the number of those killed amounted to a million, and they were thrown into lakes and mountains.

text_original: Yet, for all this, Chieh could not evade the punishment from ghosts and spirits.
text_normalized: Yet, for all this, Chieh could not evade the punishment from ghosts and spirits.

text_original: This is why I say even wealth and numbers, daring and strength, strong armour and sharp weapons cannot combat the punishment from ghosts and spirits.
text_normalized: This is why I say even wealth and numbers, daring and strength, strong armour and sharp weapons cannot combat the punishment from ghosts and spirits.

text_original: Not only is this so.
text_normalized: Not only is this so.

text_original: Anciently, King Chow of Yin was also an emperor in honour and possessed the whole empire in wealth.
text_normalized: Anciently, King Chow of Yin was also an emperor in honour and possessed the whole empire in wealth.

text_original: He cursed Heaven and blasphemed against the spirits above and destroyed the mii].titiides below.
text_normalized: He cursed Heaven and blasphemed against the spirits above and destroyed the mii].titiides below.

text_original: He exposed tbe aged and murdered tbe cliildren, tortured tbe innocent, and opened a pregnant woman.
text_normalized: He exposed tbe aged and murdered tbe cliildren, tortured tbe innocent, and opened a pregnant woman.

text_original: The common people and the widows and the widowers cried aloud, but were not heard.
text_normalized: The common people and the widows and the widowers cried aloud, but were not heard.

text_original: Thereupon Heaven commissioned King Wu to carry out the judicious punishment.
text_normalized: Thereupon Heaven commissioned King Wu to carry out the judicious punishment.

text_original: With a hundred selected chariots and four hundred warriors King Wu appointed his officials and reviewed his forces.
text_normalized: With a hundred selected chariots and four hundred warriors King Wu appointed his officials and reviewed his forces.

text_original: He battled the armies of Yin in the Wilderness of Mu.
text_normalized: He battled the armies of Yin in the Wilderness of Mu.

text_original: He captured Fei Chung and E Lai, and the multitude deserted and ran away.
text_normalized: He captured Fei Chung and E Lai, and the multitude deserted and ran away.

text_original: King Wu rushed into the palace.
text_normalized: King Wu rushed into the palace.

text_original: He executed Chow and hung him on a red ring with his crimes published on a white flag, to punish him for the feudal lords in the empire.
text_normalized: He executed Chow and hung him on a red ring with his crimes published on a white flag, to punish him for the feudal lords in the empire.

text_original: Now King Chow of Yin was an emperor in honour and possessed the whole empire in wealth.
text_normalized: Now King Chow of Yin was an emperor in honour and possessed the whole empire in wealth.

text_original: He had men of such dariag and strength as Fei Chung,  E Lai, and Duke Hu of Ch’ung, who could kill a man at the move of a finger ; and the number of those killed amounted to a million, and they were thrown into the lakes and mountains.
text_normalized: He had men of such dariag and strength as Fei Chung,  E Lai, and Duke Hu of Ch’ung, who could kill a man at the move of a finger ; and the number of those killed amounted to a million, and they were thrown into the lakes and mountains.

text_original: Yet with all these Chow could not evade the punishment from the ghosts and spirits.
text_normalized: Yet with all these Chow could not evade the punishment from the ghosts and spirits.

text_original: This is why I say even wealth and numbers, daring and strength, strong armour and sharp weapons cannot frustrate the punishment from ghosts and spirits.
text_normalized: This is why I say even wealth and numbers, daring and strength, strong armour and sharp weapons cannot frustrate the punishment from ghosts and spirits.

text_original: Moreover, Chin Ai has said : '' No virtue is too small ; no extermination of a lineage is too big.’’ This is to say.
text_normalized: Moreover, Chin Ai has said : '' No virtue is too small ; no extermination of a lineage is too big.’’ This is to say.

text_original: in distribution of rewards by ghosts and spirits no man is too insignificant to be rewarded for his virtue.
text_normalized: in distribution of rewards by ghosts and spirits no man is too insignificant to be rewarded for his virtue.

text_original: And in the meting out of punislinients by ghosts and spirits no man is too great to be punished.
text_normalized: And in the meting out of punislinients by ghosts and spirits no man is too great to be punished.

text_original: Those who deny the existence of spirits say, “ If on© does not work for the blessing of ones parents but work for their destruction, would one still be a filial son ?
text_normalized: Those who deny the existence of spirits say, “ If on© does not work for the blessing of ones parents but work for their destruction, would one still be a filial son ?

text_original: '' Mots© explained : The ghosts and spirits of all times may be divided into spirits of Heaven, spirits of hills and rivers, and ghosts of men after their death.
text_normalized: '' Mots© explained : The ghosts and spirits of all times may be divided into spirits of Heaven, spirits of hills and rivers, and ghosts of men after their death.

text_original: It is true that there are sons who die before their fathers, and younger brothers before their elder brothers.
text_normalized: It is true that there are sons who die before their fathers, and younger brothers before their elder brothers.

text_original: But, as the saying in the world goes : “He who is born first dies first.’’ So then those who die first would be the mother if not the father, and the elder sister if not the elder brother.
text_normalized: But, as the saying in the world goes : “He who is born first dies first.’’ So then those who die first would be the mother if not the father, and the elder sister if not the elder brother.

text_original: At any rate, we should prepare clean cakes and wine reverently to do sacrifice.
text_normalized: At any rate, we should prepare clean cakes and wine reverently to do sacrifice.

text_original: If ghosts and spirits do exist, then it is to serve father and mother, elder sisters and elder brothers with food and drink.
text_normalized: If ghosts and spirits do exist, then it is to serve father and mother, elder sisters and elder brothers with food and drink.

text_original: Is not this a great blessing ?
text_normalized: Is not this a great blessing ?

text_original: If ghosts and spirits did not exist it would seem to be a waste of the material for the cakes and wine.
text_normalized: If ghosts and spirits did not exist it would seem to be a waste of the material for the cakes and wine.

text_original: But such use is not just to throw it into the ditch or gully.
text_normalized: But such use is not just to throw it into the ditch or gully.

text_original: For the relatives from the clan and friends from the village and district can yet eat and drink them.
text_normalized: For the relatives from the clan and friends from the village and district can yet eat and drink them.

text_original: So, even if there were really no ghosts and spirits, a sacrifice will yet gather together a party and the participants can enjoy themselves and befriend the neighbours.
text_normalized: So, even if there were really no ghosts and spirits, a sacrifice will yet gather together a party and the participants can enjoy themselves and befriend the neighbours.

text_original: Those who hold there are no ghosts say : “Of course there are no ghosts and spirits and there-fore I should not expend my wealth on the cakes and wine and victims.
text_normalized: Those who hold there are no ghosts say : “Of course there are no ghosts and spirits and there-fore I should not expend my wealth on the cakes and wine and victims.

text_original: This is not because I am miserly about my wealth on the cakes and wine and victims.
text_normalized: This is not because I am miserly about my wealth on the cakes and wine and victims.

text_original: But (because I do not see) what I can accomplish with it.” This is opposed to the records of the sage-kings above and opposed to the practice among the filial sons among the people.
text_normalized: But (because I do not see) what I can accomplish with it.” This is opposed to the records of the sage-kings above and opposed to the practice among the filial sons among the people.

text_original: Yet they claim to be superior men in the empire.
text_normalized: Yet they claim to be superior men in the empire.

text_original: This is no way to be superior men.
text_normalized: This is no way to be superior men.

text_original: But Motse said : For me to offer sacrifice is not to throw it into the ditch or the gully.
text_normalized: But Motse said : For me to offer sacrifice is not to throw it into the ditch or the gully.

text_original: It is to bless the ghosts above and gather a party and enjoy ourselves and befriend the neighbours below.
text_normalized: It is to bless the ghosts above and gather a party and enjoy ourselves and befriend the neighbours below.

text_original: And if spirits exist, I would be serving my father and mother and brother with food.
text_normalized: And if spirits exist, I would be serving my father and mother and brother with food.

text_original: Is this not a great blessing in the world ?
text_normalized: Is this not a great blessing in the world ?

text_original: Therefore Motse said : If the rulers and the gentlemen of the world really desire to procure benefits for the world and eliminate its calamities they must believe in and teach the existence of ghosts and spirits.
text_normalized: Therefore Motse said : If the rulers and the gentlemen of the world really desire to procure benefits for the world and eliminate its calamities they must believe in and teach the existence of ghosts and spirits.

text_original: This is the way of the sage-kings.
text_normalized: This is the way of the sage-kings.
